claimText: The International Energy Agency (IEA) was founded in 1974 with the primary mandate of ensuring the security of oil supplies.
evidenceExcerpt: The International Energy Agency (IEA), located in Paris, France, was founded in 1974 to ensure the security of oil supplies.
examUse: Statement-based question on the historical mandate and origin of the IEA.
distractorHooks: Founded to promote renewable energy transition
Established to regulate global oil prices like OPEC

claimText: India is an associate member of the International Energy Agency (IEA), not a full member.
evidenceExcerpt: It has 32 Member countries and 13 Association countries, with India being an associate member.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's membership status in global energy bodies.
distractorHooks: India is a founding member of the IEA
India is a full member of the IEA

claimText: A country must be a member of the OECD to be eligible for full membership in the International Energy Agency (IEA).
evidenceExcerpt: To be a full member, a country must be an OECD member
examUse: Statement-based question on IEA membership criteria.
distractorHooks: Any UN member state can join the IEA
G20 membership is a prerequisite for IEA membership

claimText: IEA full membership requires a country to maintain crude oil reserves equivalent to 90 days of its previous year's net imports.
evidenceExcerpt: maintain crude oil reserves equivalent to 90 days of the previous year's net imports
examUse: Statement-based question on strategic petroleum reserve obligations for IEA members.
distractorHooks: Reserves equivalent to 60 days of net imports
Reserves equivalent to 90 days of total domestic consumption

claimText: To become a full member of the IEA, a country must have a demand restraint programme capable of reducing national oil consumption by up to 10%.
evidenceExcerpt: and have a demand restraint programme to reduce oil consumption by up to 10%.
examUse: Statement-based question on the demand-side management criteria for IEA membership.
distractorHooks: Programme to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 10%
Programme to reduce oil consumption by 25%

claimText: The World Energy Outlook, Global Methane Tracker, and Global EV Outlook are flagship publications of the International Energy Agency (IEA).
evidenceExcerpt: Flagship publications include the World Energy Outlook, Global Methane Tracker, Delivering Sustainable Fuels, and Global EV Outlook.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching international reports to their publishing organizations.
distractorHooks: Published by the World Economic Forum (WEF)
Published by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
